title: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedro
stakeholder_needs: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedroom. It should cover at least 25 square meters, operate below 35 dB in sleep mode, and comply with EU safety and environmental regulations.
analysis: Stakeholder needs implied by the request span: (1) End user: effective removal of PM2.5/PM10/allergens in a bedroom-sized space, very low perceived noise at night, no unwanted byproducts (ozone), simple UX (auto mode, clear air-quality and filter status), low odor/VOC annoyance reduction, and compact form factor. (2) Regulatory/compliance: EU safety and environmental compliance for a mains-powered consumer appliance (electrical safety, EMC), materials/chemical restrictions, energy/standby expectations, and clear, non-misleading performance claims (CADR/ACH definition). (3) Business/product management: clear separation of baseline vs premium (no connectivity/VOC sensor in baseline to avoid added compliance and lifecycle burdens), definable measurable requirements (CADR >=250 m³/h in max mode; sleep <=35 dB(A)), and cost/risk control by excluding UV-C and ionization. (4) Service/operations: filter replacement ecosystem (pre/HEPA/carbon), reliable filter-life estimation, and documentation/labeling to support compliance and customer support. Key requirement tensions to manage: achieving 250–300 m³/h CADR in a compact device while keeping sleep mode under 35 dB(A); defining test conditions/standards for CADR and noise; and specifying exactly which EU directives/standards and eco requirements apply to the baseline variant without connectivity.
clarification_questions: What are the target external dimensions/weight constraints for “compact” (e.g., max height/footprint), and any aesthetic constraints for bedroom use?
Which EU markets and mains conditions should be supported (e.g., 220–240 V, 50 Hz only), and is the product intended to be double-insulated (Class II) or earthed (Class I)?
What performance and test standards should define CADR and acoustics (e.g., AHAM AC-1 / GB-T 18801 for CADR; ISO 3744/3746 for sound), and should claims be validated by an independent lab?

Eliminate backup cell in baseline; if required, add reverse polarity + series resistor; add leak containment pocket and conformal coat keep-out
Design review action logged; supplier shortlist created
Avoid unnecessary batteries to reduce chemical/compliance risk; prefer nonvolatile memory for settings
Baseline target: no user-replaceable batteries to simplify WEEE/labeling
Provide airflow to meet coverage >=25 m² and CADR target while ensuring reliability
Bearing wear leading to increased friction or seizure
Reduced airflow; CADR below claim; overheating; noise increase; potential smoke odor; field failures

End-of-line current/noise check; life test at high dust loading; vibration analysis
Add pre-filter efficiency requirement; improve motor cooling path; tighten imbalance tolerance; add runtime-based predictive service alert
Draft motor spec updated; test plan drafted
Dust management is critical to motor life; specify service interval based on real dust loading
Ensure max-mode CADR and sleep-mode <35 dB(A) are both validated across tolerance
Control fan speed across modes; protect against overcurrent/overtemp; maintain EMC compliance
Fan runs uncontrolled or stops; overheating; potential fire hazard; noncompliance with safety
Voltage transients; insufficient creepage/clearance; inadequate derating; ESD; solder voids
Derating rules; TVS; proper layout creepage/clearance; conformal coat; AOI + X-ray for power parts
HiPot; functional test; thermal imaging at max load; EMC pre-scan
Add redundant thermal cutoff (non-resettable) on heatsink area; tighten surge spec; add inrush limiting
PCB layout checklist applied; safety review scheduled
Safety-critical power path needs independent fail-safe cutoff
Applies to EU LVD/EN 60335 safety evidence
Allow adequate inlet flow while preventing finger access and large debris ingress
Airflow drops; CADR below spec; motor load increases; noise rises; user dissatisfaction
Inlet area too small; no stand-off; device used against wall/bedside fabric; poor user guidance
Increase inlet free area; add stand-off feet; add blockage detection via differential pressure or current signature
Airflow test with obstruction scenarios; user manual warnings; EOL airflow margin check
Add inlet stand-off geometry >=15 mm; implement blockage warning on UI; add top/bottom intake path to reduce single-point blockage
Concept sketches created; obstruction test matrix defined
Real bedroom use includes soft furnishings; design for misuse tolerance
Maintain safety finger probe compliance while increasing open area
Remove PM2.5/PM10/allergens to meet performance claims without bypass
Filter media tear or poor pleat bonding
Particle breakthrough; health impact; claim noncompliance; customer complaints
Supplier process variation; handling damage; insufficient potting; vibration during shipping

Incoming AQL; CADR verification on samples; leak scan (aerosol) on pilot lots
Add 100% gasket integrity check at assembly; add shipping corner protectors; tighten supplier control plan
Supplier audit scheduled; draft IQC checklist prepared
HEPA integrity issues often originate in handling; packaging is part of quality
Ensure no ozone/ionization; performance solely via filtration
Reduce odor/VOC annoyance without excessive pressure drop or dusting
Black dust deposition; user irritation; perceived low quality; potential sensor fouling
Low-grade carbon; insufficient binder; abrasion during transport; high airflow velocity
Specify low-dust carbon pellet/impregnated media; add downstream nonwoven; packaging vibration control
Dust shedding test; visual inspection; customer-use simulation
Add dust capture scrim layer; revise acceptance criteria with gravimetric dust limit; improve handling SOP
Material samples ordered; test fixture defined
Odor control features must not create new visible contamination problems
Balance VOC benefit vs pressure drop to preserve CADR/noise
Prevent unfiltered air bypass around filter perimeter
Gasket compression set leading to bypass over time
Reduced filtration efficiency; CADR/clean air delivery degrades; claim risk
Wrong elastomer; overcompression; heat aging; poor tolerance stack-up; repeated filter changes
Select low-compression-set foam; define compression range; tolerance analysis; aging validation
Aerosol leak test on pilot builds; fit-check gauge; periodic field return inspection
Add hard stops to control compression; add keyed filter frame; increase gasket width; update service instructions to avoid mis-seating
Tolerance stack started; gasket material shortlist approved
Bypass is a silent failure; design in poka-yoke and controlled compression
Key to meeting PM removal performance over product life
Provide structural integrity, safe touch temperatures, and fire safety compliance
Fire propagation risk; regulatory noncompliance; recall
Incorrect resin; regrind overuse; thin wall near motor/PCB; hotspot under fault
Specify UL94 V-0 (or equivalent) material; limit regrind; thermal simulation; fault condition analysis
Material cert review; incoming material ID; glow-wire/needle-flame tests; abnormal operation tests
Increase wall thickness at hotspots; add internal heat shields; lock resin grade in drawing and supplier agreement
Material datasheets collected; compliance test slot booked
Material control is as important as design; prevent unintended substitutions
EU LVD/EN 60335 evidence must include abnormal/fault conditions
Maintain alignment and avoid rattles; ensure safe access protection
Clip fatigue or screw loosening causing rattling
Noise above 35 dB(A) in sleep mode; customer returns; perceived poor quality
Improper torque; tolerance stack; resonant modes; insufficient damping; repeated filter door access
Torque specs + control; thread-forming screw guidelines; add foam damping; modal analysis
EOL acoustic test in sleep mode; vibration test; audit torque records
Add anti-loosen features (patch/washer); redesign clips for higher fatigue margin; add acoustic baffles to reduce structure-borne noise
EOL acoustic station concept drafted; torque tool calibration plan updated
Sleep-mode noise is highly sensitive to rattles; include acoustic test at EOL
Noise requirement must be verified on production units, not only prototypes
Charger / Power supply (AC mains adapter or internal PSU)
Convert EU mains to safe low voltage; meet standby power and safety requirements
Insulation breakdown leading to electric shock
User injury; fatality; regulatory noncompliance; recall
Creepage/clearance violation; poor transformer quality; humidity ingress; counterfeit adapter
Use certified PSU (EN/IEC 61558/62368 as applicable) or internal PSU per EN 60335; supplier approval list; sealed design
HiPot 100%; dielectric withstand; supplier COC verification; incoming authentication
Mandate only approved PSU part numbers; add adapter ID labeling; add moisture protection; implement anti-counterfeit packaging
Supplier certification reviewed; incoming inspection plan drafted
Rely on certified power components and robust incoming controls
Ensure correct EU plug types and markings for target markets
Prevent cord pull-out and maintain protective insulation integrity
Strain relief failure or cord jacket damage
Exposed conductors; shock hazard; intermittent power; overheating at contact
Insufficient strain relief design; sharp edges; poor molding; repeated bending in bedroom use
Design per pull/bend standards; add grommet; edge radiusing; select flexible cord spec
Cord pull test; bend test; visual inspection; EOL tug test
Increase strain relief length; add internal clamp; implement 100% tug test; update cord routing to avoid pinch points
Routing updated in CAD; test samples ordered
User handling of cords is harsh; design for repeated bending near entry
Coordinate with PSU choice (external adapter vs internal PSU)
Allow mode selection (sleep/auto/max) and clear status without confusion
Sleep mode not actually engaged or inadvertently exits
Noise exceeds 35 dB(A) at night; user dissatisfaction; returns; negative reviews
Ambiguous UI; accidental button press; firmware state error; inadequate debounce; power glitch
Clear sleep indicator; long-press to toggle; debounce; state persistence; UX validation
EOL functional test of sleep mode RPM; firmware unit tests; usability study
Add dedicated sleep button and indicator; implement lock-out (child lock) option; store last mode in NVM; add watchdog reset handling
UI flow drafted; firmware requirements updated
Noise requirement depends on correct mode control; UI clarity prevents misuse
Ensure brightness dimming in sleep mode to reduce perceived disturbance
Air quality indicator (PM sensor if included in auto mode)
Provide accurate PM-based auto control and user feedback without misleading claims
Sensor drift or contamination causing incorrect readings
Auto mode under-cleans room; user distrust; performance claim disputes
Dust accumulation; VOC cross-sensitivity; fan turbulence near sensor inlet; aging
Place sensor in representative flow; add sensor inlet filter; calibration strategy; periodic self-check
Correlation testing vs reference instrument; firmware plausibility checks; service diagnostics
Add sensor flow isolation duct; implement drift detection and fallback control; provide user messaging for sensor cleaning/service
Sensor placement DOE planned; reference instrument procured
If sensor is used for claims/UI, validate under real aerosol types and humidity
Baseline may omit sensor; if omitted, remove air-quality numeric display to avoid misleading impression
Maintain low pressure drop and avoid whistling tones across airflow range
Flow-induced tonal noise (whistle) in sleep mode
Perceived noise exceeds 35 dB(A) or is annoying; returns; failed acoustic targets
Sharp edges; narrow slots; resonance cavity; manufacturing variation; grille geometry
Acoustic CFD; radius edges; avoid slot aspect ratios that whistle; add damping foam strategically
Anechoic chamber test; near-field microphone scan; production part acoustic audit
Redesign inlet/outlet edges with larger radii; add internal perforated diffuser; tighten grille tooling tolerances
Whistle risk review completed; prototypes planned
Tonal noise is more disturbing than broadband; validate psychoacoustic metrics, not only dB(A)
Include worst-case tolerance stack parts in acoustic validation
Prevent air leakage that reduces performance and causes dust escape
Filter door not fully latched; leakage path opens
Bypass reduces clean air delivery; dust leaks; rattling; safety risk if door opens
Weak latch; no positive feedback; warpage; user error during filter replacement
Robust latch with tactile click; door-closed interlock switch; warpage control; user instructions
Door-closed detection in firmware; EOL latch force test; drop test
Add interlock to prevent fan operation if door open; add latch redundancy; add clear pictograms for filter install
Interlock concept approved; latch DOE scheduled
Serviceability features must include poka-yoke to maintain filtration integrity
Interlock must be designed to avoid easy defeat while still user-friendly

versions.stakeholder_needs: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedroom. It should cover at least 25 square meters, operate below 35 dB in sleep mode, and comply with EU safety and environmental regulations.

messages.content: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedroom. It should cover at least 25 square meters, operate below 35 dB in sleep mode, and comply with EU safety and environmental regulations.

Stakeholders include: end users (bedroom occupants needing quiet, effective purification and good UX), buyers (price/size aesthetics), regulatory bodies (EU safety, EMC, RoHS/REACH, WEEE, Ecodesign where applicable), and service/manufacturing (filter availability, reliability, maintainability). Key requirements must translate stated constraints (>=25 m² coverage, <35 dB sleep) into measurable specs (CADR, noise measurement method), plus define compliance scope, operating conditions, and consumables (filters). Risks: ambiguous “cover” metric, undefined pollutants/targets, and unclear regulatory categories (ionizers/ozone, Wi‑Fi radio).
Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedroom. It should cover at least 25 square meters, operate below 35 dB in sleep mode, and comply with EU safety and environmental regulations.

Stakeholder needs implied by the request span: (1) End user: effective removal of PM2.5/PM10/allergens in a bedroom-sized space, very low perceived noise at night, no unwanted byproducts (ozone), simple UX (auto mode, clear air-quality and filter status), low odor/VOC annoyance reduction, and compact form factor. (2) Regulatory/compliance: EU safety and environmental compliance for a mains-powered consumer appliance (electrical safety, EMC), materials/chemical restrictions, energy/standby expectations, and clear, non-misleading performance claims (CADR/ACH definition). (3) Business/product management: clear separation of baseline vs premium (no connectivity/VOC sensor in baseline to avoid added compliance and lifecycle burdens), definable measurable requirements (CADR >=250 m³/h in max mode; sleep <=35 dB(A)), and cost/risk control by excluding UV-C and ionization. (4) Service/operations: filter replacement ecosystem (pre/HEPA/carbon), reliable filter-life estimation, and documentation/labeling to support compliance and customer support. Key requirement tensions to manage: achieving 250–300 m³/h CADR in a compact device while keeping sleep mode under 35 dB(A); defining test conditions/standards for CADR and noise; and specifying exactly which EU directives/standards and eco requirements apply to the baseline variant without connectivity.
